AIControls.name: AIControls
AIControls.description: Controls access to AI features: Translations, PDF alt text generation, smart tab groups, display summaries in link previews, AI chatbot in the sidebar, and smart windows. The Default value applies to all AI features unless overridden by a specific feature.
AIControls.security_privacy_impact: Content sent to the AI chatbot, translations, or link-preview features may leave the device for processing (locally or in the cloud, depending on the feature/provider) - review what each feature sends before enabling it for users who handle sensitive data.

BlockAboutConfig.name: BlockAboutConfig
BlockAboutConfig.security_privacy_impact: Reduces the risk of users (or malware/social-engineering "paste this into your browser" scams) weakening security-relevant preferences by hand; also blocks legitimate troubleshooting via about:config.

DNSOverHTTPS.name: DNSOverHTTPS
DNSOverHTTPS.security_privacy_impact: Encrypting DNS prevents on-path eavesdroppers (e.g. a hostile local network) from seeing which domains a user resolves, but can also bypass enterprise DNS-based filtering/blocklists unless configured to use an approved resolver.

DisableAppUpdate.name: DisableAppUpdate
DisableAppUpdate.security_privacy_impact: Disabling updates leaves known, publicly disclosed vulnerabilities unpatched - only do this if updates are managed through another controlled channel.

DisableDeveloperTools.name: DisableDeveloperTools
DisableDeveloperTools.description: Disable access to the browser developer tools.
DisableDeveloperTools.security_privacy_impact: Closes off a common vector for "paste this code into the console" social-engineering attacks and casual inspection of page scripts, at the cost of blocking legitimate debugging.

DisableFirefoxAccounts.name: DisableFirefoxAccounts
DisableFirefoxAccounts.security_privacy_impact: Prevents bookmarks, history, saved passwords, and open tabs from being synced to Mozilla's servers and other signed-in devices.

DisableFirefoxStudies.name: DisableFirefoxStudies
DisableFirefoxStudies.security_privacy_impact: Opts out of Normandy/Shield, Mozilla's remote-experimentation system that can push configuration or behavior changes to the browser without a separate update.

DisableForgetButton.name: DisableForgetButton
DisableForgetButton.description: Disable the "Forget" button used to quickly clear recent history.
DisableForgetButton.security_privacy_impact: Removing this button takes away a quick, one-click way for users to clear recent history/cookies themselves - a privacy convenience feature, not a risk in itself.

DisableFormHistory.name: DisableFormHistory
DisableFormHistory.description: Disable saving of form and search bar entry history.
DisableFormHistory.security_privacy_impact: Form and search-bar entries can include sensitive typed data (names, addresses, partial credentials); disabling history prevents it from being retained locally.

DisablePocket.name: DisablePocket
DisablePocket.security_privacy_impact: Prevents URLs a user saves for later from being sent to Pocket, a separate Mozilla-operated third-party service.

DisablePrivateBrowsing.name: DisablePrivateBrowsing
DisablePrivateBrowsing.security_privacy_impact: Removing private browsing takes away a privacy option (no local history/cookie retention for that window) rather than closing a security gap.

DisableTelemetry.name: DisableTelemetry
DisableTelemetry.security_privacy_impact: Stops usage and diagnostic data from being sent to Mozilla; has no effect on data sent to websites themselves.

DisabledCiphers.name: DisabledCiphers
DisabledCiphers.description: Disable (or explicitly enable) specific TLS cryptographic ciphers.
DisabledCiphers.security_privacy_impact: Disabling weak/legacy TLS cipher suites hardens the browser against downgrade and cryptographic attacks; over-restricting can break TLS to servers that only offer older ciphers.

DisplayBookmarksToolbar.name: DisplayBookmarksToolbar
DisplayBookmarksToolbar.description: Show the bookmarks toolbar by default.

EnableTrackingProtection.name: EnableTrackingProtection
EnableTrackingProtection.security_privacy_impact: Blocks known third-party trackers and cross-site tracking scripts, directly reducing ad-tech/analytics tracking of users across sites.

EncryptedMediaExtensions.name: EncryptedMediaExtensions
EncryptedMediaExtensions.description: Configure whether encrypted media extensions (e.g. Widevine) may be downloaded.
EncryptedMediaExtensions.security_privacy_impact: Allowing EME (e.g. Widevine) lets closed-source, third-party-signed DRM code run with elevated media-pipeline access - a supply-chain/attack-surface tradeoff for DRM-protected content support.

ExtensionSettings.name: ExtensionSettings
ExtensionSettings.description: Manage which extensions can/must be installed.
ExtensionSettings.security_privacy_impact: One of the highest-impact security controls available: extensions can read/modify every page a user visits and access browsing data, so which origins/IDs are allowed, blocked, or force-installed directly determines the browser's attack surface.

ExtensionUpdate.name: ExtensionUpdate
ExtensionUpdate.description: Enable or disable automatic extension updates.
ExtensionUpdate.security_privacy_impact: Disabling automatic extension updates leaves installed extensions unpatched against vulnerabilities discovered after installation.

FirefoxHome.name: FirefoxHome
FirefoxHome.description: Customize the Firefox Home (New Tab) page.

GenerativeAI.name: GenerativeAI
GenerativeAI.description: Configure generative AI features (chatbot sidebar, link-preview key points, smart tab groups, smart window). Set Enabled to false to turn off all generative AI features at once; set Locked to true to prevent users from changing these settings themselves.
GenerativeAI.security_privacy_impact: Content processed by these AI features (chatbot, link previews, tab grouping) may be sent off-device for processing - review what each feature sends before enabling it for users who handle sensitive data.

IPProtectionAvailable.name: IPProtectionAvailable
IPProtectionAvailable.description: Control whether Firefox's built-in IP Protection (VPN) is available to users. When disabled, the feature is turned off and cannot be used.
IPProtectionAvailable.security_privacy_impact: Controls a user-facing privacy feature (masking the browser's IP from sites via a built-in VPN); disabling it removes a privacy option and, on networks that require unencrypted-through-VPN traffic for monitoring/compliance, avoids that traffic being tunneled around network controls.

InstallAddonsPermission.name: InstallAddonsPermission
InstallAddonsPermission.description: Configure which origins may prompt to install extensions.
InstallAddonsPermission.security_privacy_impact: Controls which origins may prompt users to install an extension; overly permissive settings increase the risk of drive-by or social-engineered malicious extension installs.

NetworkPrediction.name: NetworkPrediction
NetworkPrediction.description: Enable or disable network prediction (DNS prefetching, TCP/TLS preconnect).
NetworkPrediction.security_privacy_impact: Prefetching/preconnecting can reveal a user's likely next destination to network observers and third-party origins before the user actually navigates there.

PasswordManagerEnabled.name: PasswordManagerEnabled
PasswordManagerEnabled.description: Enable or disable the built-in password manager.
PasswordManagerEnabled.security_privacy_impact: Disabling the built-in password manager may push users toward less secure habits (password reuse, browser-external plaintext notes) unless a managed alternative is provided in its place.

Permissions.name: Permissions
Permissions.description: Configure per-feature site permission defaults (Camera, Microphone, Autoplay, Notifications, etc.).
Permissions.security_privacy_impact: Directly governs default access to sensitive capabilities - camera, microphone, notifications, autoplay - so overly permissive defaults increase the risk of unwanted mic/camera access or notification-based phishing.

PopupBlocking.name: PopupBlocking
PopupBlocking.security_privacy_impact: Pop-ups are a common vector for malvertising, fake-download prompts, and scareware; blocking them by default reduces that attack surface.

Preferences.name: Preferences
Preferences.security_privacy_impact: Sets arbitrary preference values directly, which can include preferences that themselves control security behavior - review each preference set through this policy for unintended side effects.

Proxy.name: Proxy
Proxy.security_privacy_impact: Misconfiguration can either leak traffic around required corporate monitoring/filtering, or - if pointed at an untrusted proxy - route all browsing traffic through a third party able to observe or tamper with it.

SSLVersionMin.name: SSLVersionMin
SSLVersionMin.description: Set the minimum allowed TLS/SSL version.
SSLVersionMin.security_privacy_impact: Enforces a floor on the TLS/SSL version the browser will negotiate, directly preventing downgrade to older protocol versions with known cryptographic weaknesses.

SanitizeOnShutdown.name: SanitizeOnShutdown
SanitizeOnShutdown.description: Configure which browsing data is cleared automatically on shutdown.
SanitizeOnShutdown.security_privacy_impact: Automatically clearing history/cookies/cache on exit reduces the local forensic footprint of a browsing session, particularly useful on shared or kiosk machines.

SearchSuggestEnabled.name: SearchSuggestEnabled
SearchSuggestEnabled.security_privacy_impact: While enabled, each keystroke typed in the address/search bar can be sent to the default search engine as a suggestion query, leaking partial search intent to that third party as it is typed.

UserMessaging.name: UserMessaging
UserMessaging.description: Configure in-product recommendations and messaging shown to the user.
